aboutDescription: My name is Ulugbek Nazarov. I am a frontend developer focused on JavaScript, React, and TypeScript. I have been programming for about six months, and during this time I have not only studied theory but also actively applied my knowledge in practice by developing projects and improving my skills. I care deeply about how interfaces look and feel. I pay attention to details and strive to create solutions that are not only functional but also visually clean and user-friendly. For me, good frontend development is when code and design work together as one. I work with React and TypeScript, write clean and understandable code, constantly explore new approaches, and aim to improve the quality of my projects. All my work can be found on GitHub, reflecting my current level and progress. Right now, I continue growing as a developer, deepening my knowledge and gaining experience to build more complex and polished interfaces.
html: I build clear and semantically structured page layouts. I think not only about how an interface looks, but also about how it works internally.
css: I create clean and responsive interfaces. I work with Flexbox, Grid, and pay close attention to details to keep everything modern and polished.
git: I use Git for version control, work with repositories, and maintain a clean and organized commit history.
js: I add interactivity and logic to interfaces. I work with the DOM, events, and make websites feel dynamic and responsive.
figma: I design interfaces and plan the visual side before development. I focus on composition, spacing, and user experience.
npm: I use npm to manage dependencies and configure projects. I connect libraries, organize the development environment, and simplify workflows.
react: I build interfaces from reusable components. I work with hooks, state management, and application logic to create scalable frontend solutions.
scss: I structure styles using variables, mixins, and nesting. I write maintainable and scalable styling code.
ts: I write more reliable and predictable code using static typing. This helps avoid errors and better structure projects.
